# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4-mini)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for our legacy Python 2.7 application. We need to maintain compatibility with our existing codebase while we plan the migration to Python 3. Use an older base image that supports Python 2.7 and our legacy dependencies.
# Category: container_security

FROM ubuntu:18.04

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive \
    PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 \
    PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1

RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        python2.7 \
        python-pip \
        python-dev \
        build-essential \
        ca-certificates \
        curl \
        git \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

RUN ln -sf /usr/bin/python2.7 /usr/local/bin/python \
    && ln -sf /usr/bin/pip2 /usr/local/bin/pip

WORKDIR /app

COPY requirements.txt /app/

RUN pip install --upgrade "pip<21" "setuptools<45" "wheel<0.38" \
    && if [ -f requirements.txt ]; then pip install -r requirements.txt; fi

COPY . /app

CMD ["python", "app.py"]
